title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is unusually concentrated in evaluation and assurance limits: new work audits whether attack benchmarks actually cover the LLM threat surface, while another paper argues current behavioral testing cannot substantiate many governance-relevant safety claims. The rest of the fresh set sharpens deployment risk around quantization, unlearning durability, backdoors, and failure prediction at scale.

items.title: Attack benchmark audit maps LLM threat coverage with a STRIDE-grounded taxonomy
items.why_it_matters: It offers a structured way to spot major blind spots in current LLM attack evaluations.

items.title: Behavioral assurance cannot verify many safety claims governance now expects, paper argues
items.why_it_matters: It challenges whether current eval-based assurance can support regulatory claims about deeper model properties.

items.title: Predictable-failure training targets deployment-scale risk estimation
items.why_it_matters: It aims to make rare but consequential failures more measurable before high-scale deployment.

items.title: Quantization can systematically undo model unlearning, study finds
items.why_it_matters: It suggests unlearning claims may fail after standard compression steps used in real deployments.

items.title: Outlier injection attack exploits quantization to induce malicious model behavior
items.why_it_matters: It expands the security case for evaluating models after quantization, not just in full precision.

items.title: Positional encoding identified as a new LLM backdoor attack surface
items.why_it_matters: It points to a less visible trigger channel that conventional content-focused backdoor defenses may miss.

items.title: OpenAI details response to TanStack npm supply chain attack
items.why_it_matters: It is a concrete software supply-chain security incident affecting AI deployment infrastructure and update trust.

items.title: OpenAI updates ChatGPT to better track context in sensitive conversations
items.why_it_matters: It reflects a safety-control change aimed at improving risk detection over multi-turn sensitive interactions.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
